urn:contentItem:6JHD-YW33-RS13-J2JC-00000-00Climate activists block governor's mansion, call Stein 'complicit' with Duke Energy2026-05-06T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:07:21ZLexisNexisCLIMATE CHANGE NEWSClimate activists block governor's mansion, call Stein 'complicit' with Duke EnergyJosh Shaffer

Climate activists blocked the entrance to the Governor's Mansion Wednesday, calling for an end to Duke Energy's fossil-fuel expansion and accusing Gov. Josh Stein of being "complicit" in policies that intensify hurricanes, wildfires and drought.

By 3 p.m., six activists associated with Durham-based nonprofit NC Warn had occupied the Blount Street gate for three hours, resisting three requests to move from Capitol police. They later moved to a lower and more heavily trafficked gate on Jones Street.

No arrests have yet been made.

"We are here in good conscience," said Jim Warren, executive director of NC Warn. "We ask that the government tell the truth about Duke Energy as a climate polluter. ... Instead, he keeps repeating the corporate propaganda."

NC Warn pointed to Duke Energy's planned 12,000 megawatt fossil fuel expansion as part of a 50-year strategy to ignore climate change data - including its own research - while touting plans to become carbon-neutral by 2050.

Warren said the 2050 plan is "bogus" and irrelevant considering the damage North Carolina will suffer in the next two decades.

Protesters also pointed to Duke calling for a rate hike while earning nearly $5 billion in 2025.

Stein has opposed this hike and accused Duke of backing away from clean energy policies.

"Duke Energy's proposed rate hike is simply too high and comes as the company is also retreating on more affordable clean energy," Stein said in December. "At a time when families are struggling to make ends meet, we should be doing everything we can to make life more affordable, not less. I will continue to fight on behalf of every North Carolinian to lower costs and grow the economy."

Asked Wednesday, a spokesperson said the Governor has vetoed the General Assembly's bill that would increase utility bills, shift costs of industrial users to residential ratepayers and slow efforts to bring down emissions.

"After lawmakers overrode his veto," the spokesperson said, "Governor Stein supported Attorney General Jackson's efforts to challenge Duke Energy's excessive rate hikes and called on the Utilities Commission to require significant changes to Duke Energy's Carolinas Resource Plan, which retreated on affordable clean energy. The state needs an energy system that protects consumers, reduces pollution, and supports economic growth."

Warren on Wednesday called the governor's effort insufficient and said Duke is "forcing us to pay for our own demise."

More than 60 scientists wrote an open letter to Stein in April warning of "climate catastrophe," urging him to speak out on Duke's reliance on fossil fuels and reluctance to transition to renewable energy.

At a rally before the six protesters blocked the gate, roughly 20 more joined the chorus for louder environmental truth.

"As the hurricane season rapidly approaches, the stress of living in what has become Hurricane Alley haunts us," said Bobby Jones, an environmental justice advocate from Goldsboro. "We should no longer be forced by Duke Energy to finance our own demise."

Duke Energy did not issue a response.

urn:contentItem:6JHD-YW33-RS13-J2KR-00000-00Climate activists block governor's mansion, call Stein 'complicit' with Duke Energy2026-05-06T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:07:21ZLexisNexisCLIMATE CHANGE NEWSClimate activists block governor's mansion, call Stein 'complicit' with Duke EnergyJosh Shaffer

Climate activists blocked the entrance to the Governor's Mansion Wednesday, calling for an end to Duke Energy's fossil-fuel expansion and accusing Gov. Josh Stein of being "complicit" in policies that intensify hurricanes, wildfires and drought.

By 3 p.m., six activists associated with Durham-based nonprofit NC Warn had occupied the Blount Street gate for three hours, resisting three requests to move from Capitol police. They later moved to a lower and more heavily trafficked gate on Jones Street.

No arrests have yet been made.

"We are here in good conscience," said Jim Warren, executive director of NC Warn. "We ask that the government tell the truth about Duke Energy as a climate polluter. ... Instead, he keeps repeating the corporate propaganda."

Warren said the 2050 plan is "bogus" and irrelevant considering the damage North Carolina will suffer in the next two decades.

Protesters also pointed to Duke calling for a rate hike while earning nearly $5 billion in 2025.

Stein has opposed this hike and accused Duke of backing away from clean energy policies.

"Duke Energy's proposed rate hike is simply too high and comes as the company is also retreating on more affordable clean energy," Stein said in December. I will continue to fight on behalf of every North Carolinian to lower costs and grow the economy."

"After lawmakers overrode his veto," the spokesperson said, "Governor Stein supported Attorney General Jackson's efforts to challenge Duke Energy's excessive rate hikes and called on the Utilities Commission to require significant changes to Duke Energy's Carolinas Resource Plan, which retreated on affordable clean energy. The state needs an energy system that protects consumers, reduces pollution, and supports economic growth."

Warren on Wednesday called the governor's effort insufficient and said Duke is "forcing us to pay for our own demise."

Fri Apr 24, 2026: Duke Energy Secures 20-Year License for Robinson Nuclear Plant

Duke Energy Corporation has received a significant boost with the U.S. Nuclear Regulatory Commission's (NRC) decision to grant a 20-year extension for the operating license of its Robinson Nuclear Plant. This renewal allows the facility to continue producing energy until the year 2050, a crucial development for the utility giant. The announcement was made on April 23rd, 2026, and signifies Duke Energy's commitment to providing stable and sustainable energy to the region.

The Robinson Nuclear Plant, located in Hartsville, South Carolina, plays a vital role in Duke Energy's operations, supplying electricity to approximately 570,000 households. This license extension not only secures the plant's operational future but also aligns with Duke Energy's broader strategy of maintaining a diverse energy portfolio. The company is focused on integrating nuclear, natural gas, and renewable energy sources to meet the growing energy demands of its 8 million customers across various states, including the Carolinas, Indiana, Florida, Ohio, and Kentucky.

Duke Energy operates within the regulated utilities sector, boasting a market capitalization of approximately $99.64 billion. The company is segmented into electric utilities and infrastructure, gas utilities and infrastructure, and commercial renewables. Currently, the company's P/E ratio stands at 20.26, which is close to its five-year median, indicating that the stock may be fairly valued in the current market context. Investors are encouraged to consider this valuation alongside other financial metrics when evaluating the stock's potential.

The GF Score(TradeMark) for Duke Energy is 77 out of 100, reflecting strong performance across key metrics that can lead to higher long-term returns. The company's profitability rank is notably strong at 7 out of 10, underscoring its operational efficiency. However, concerns regarding financial strength persist, with a rating of just 3 out of 10, suggesting potential issues related to debt levels and overall financial health. Investors should carefully weigh these factors when considering Duke Energy as part of their investment portfolio.

In recent months, insider trading activity has raised eyebrows, with insiders selling $5.6 million worth of shares and no recorded insider buying. This trend may lead to questions about insider confidence in the company's future performance. Overall, Duke Energy presents a mixed outlook for investors. While the recent license renewal for the Robinson Nuclear Plant strengthens its operational foundation, its financial challenges could impact long-term growth. Investors are encouraged to explore further insights about Duke Energy and its current market position.

Recent News 2 of 3, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 395 words

Wed Apr 22, 2026: Duke Energy Foundation Launches America250 Grants for South Carolina Green Spaces

GREENVILLE, S.C.– To strengthen communities across South Carolina as the nation approaches its 250th anniversary, Duke Energy Foundation is awarding $275,000 to nonprofit organizations through a first round of funding as part of its America250 initiative. This funding, announced on 22nd April 2026, celebrates Earth Day and aims to enhance local parks, tree planting efforts, and trail maintenance projects throughout the Palmetto State.

The grants, which form part of a total investment exceeding $550,000 in South Carolina, are aimed at revitalizing parks and shared green spaces, supporting tree planting, and litter prevention initiatives. "As we look back on our country's 250-year anniversary, it's an opportunity to reflect on our nation's history while working to support the next generation," said Tim Pearson, Duke Energy South Carolina president. "We are proud to partner with local organizations who can help care for the Palmetto State now, so future generations can enjoy its beauty."

Duke Energy Foundation has awarded a total of 11 grants in this first round of funding. Notable recipients include the South Carolina Wildlife Federation, Foothills Trail Conservancy, and the Upstate Greenways & Trails Alliance. Each organization is expected to utilize the funding to enhance local environments and foster community engagement in conservation efforts. For instance, Upstate Greenways & Trails Alliance plans to create a new trail that highlights the scenic beauty of Lake Keowee while educating visitors about the Revolutionary War history in the area.

The positive responses from grant recipients highlight the importance of such initiatives. Matt Flege, Executive Director of Upstate Greenways & Trails Alliance, expressed gratitude for the support, stating, "The America250 grant will allow us to create a new trail showcasing the beauty of Lake Keowee to South Carolina residents and visitors alike." Similarly, Susan H. Wild, Chair of Sumter Green, emphasized how the investment strengthens their commitment to beautifying the community, ensuring that efforts made today will benefit future generations.

These grants mark the first awards under Duke Energy Foundation's America250 initiative, which represents a commitment of over $1 million towards community-driven projects across the company's six-state service territories. Additional grant recipients will be announced later this spring, including initiatives focused on veterans' workforce pathways and enhancing access to history and civics education. Duke Energy Foundation continues to provide nearly $30 million annually in philanthropic support to the communities it serves, reflecting its dedication to environmental stewardship and community development.

Recent News 3 of 3, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 271 words

Wed Apr 22, 2026: Duke Energy Honored with Arbor Day Foundation’s Tree Line USA Award for 20th Year

Duke Energy Corporation has received national recognition for its tree care practices for the 20th consecutive year. The Arbor Day Foundation named Duke Energy Florida a 'Tree Line USA' utility, citing the company's rigorous tree pruning standards, comprehensive employee training, and public education efforts aimed at reducing power outages and safeguarding electrical infrastructure.

On 22nd April 2026, the Arbor Day Foundation lauded Duke Energy for meeting industry standards for tree care, supporting tree planting initiatives, and engaging in annual arbor education programs. The designation reflects Duke Energy's commitment to sustainable practices, as well as its proactive approach to maintaining the health of urban trees in proximity to electrical lines.

One of Duke Energy's ongoing initiatives includes an annual free tree giveaway for customers through the Arbor Day Foundation's Energy-Saving Trees program. In 2026, the company distributed 1,000 trees to Florida customers, bringing the total number of free trees given away statewide to over 16,000 since 2017. These trees are shipped directly to customers with detailed planting instructions designed to encourage proper placement away from power lines.

Company officials noted that proper tree placement is essential for conserving energy and minimizing the risk of outages during storm and hurricane seasons. Duke Energy also conducts year-round vegetation maintenance near power lines and ramps up these efforts ahead of hurricane season, ensuring a reliable power supply for its customers.

Additionally, Duke Energy reminds customers not to trim trees near power lines themselves and encourages them to contact Sunshine 811 before digging near underground utilities. More information about responsible tree planting near electrical equipment can be found through Duke Energy's Right Tree Right Place program.

3.3 Satisfies four criteria of Benjamin Graham

- The P/E of 22.3 is 0.4 times the highest average P/E of 57.7 in the last five years. This is a value criterion, according to Benjamin Graham who described as a value criterion "A P/E ratio down to less than four-tenth of the highest average P/E ratio the stock attained in the most recent five years".

-""Earnings growth over the most recent ten years of 7% compounded - that is a doubling of earnings in a five-year period""; the earnings per share have more than doubled in the last 5 years.

-""Stability of growth in earnings over the last 5 years, defined as no more than two declines of 5% or greater in year-end earnings""; there have been 2 declines in earnings in the last 5 years.

SECTION 1 RECENT NEWS

Fri Apr 24, 2026: Duke Energy Secures 20-Year License for Robinson Nuclear Plant

Duke Energy Corporation has received a significant boost with the U.S. Nuclear Regulatory Commission's (NRC) decision to grant a 20-year extension for the operating license of its Robinson Nuclear Plant. This renewal allows the facility to continue producing energy until the year 2050, a crucial development for the utility giant. The announcement was made on April 23rd, 2026, and signifies Duke Energy's commitment to providing stable and sustainable energy to the region.

The Robinson Nuclear Plant, located in Hartsville, South Carolina, plays a vital role in Duke Energy's operations, supplying electricity to approximately 570,000 households. This license extension not only secures the plant's operational future but also aligns with Duke Energy's broader strategy of maintaining a diverse energy portfolio. The company is focused on integrating nuclear, natural gas, and renewable energy sources to meet the growing energy demands of its 8 million customers across various states, including the Carolinas, Indiana, Florida, Ohio, and Kentucky.

Duke Energy operates within the regulated utilities sector, boasting a market capitalization of approximately $99.64 billion. The company is segmented into electric utilities and infrastructure, gas utilities and infrastructure, and commercial renewables. Currently, the company's P/E ratio stands at 20.26, which is close to its five-year median, indicating that the stock may be fairly valued in the current market context. Investors are encouraged to consider this valuation alongside other financial metrics when evaluating the stock's potential.

The GF Score(TradeMark) for Duke Energy is 77 out of 100, reflecting strong performance across key metrics that can lead to higher long-term returns. The company's profitability rank is notably strong at 7 out of 10, underscoring its operational efficiency. However, concerns regarding financial strength persist, with a rating of just 3 out of 10, suggesting potential issues related to debt levels and overall financial health. Investors should carefully weigh these factors when considering Duke Energy as part of their investment portfolio.

In recent months, insider trading activity has raised eyebrows, with insiders selling $5.6 million worth of shares and no recorded insider buying. This trend may lead to questions about insider confidence in the company's future performance. Overall, Duke Energy presents a mixed outlook for investors. While the recent license renewal for the Robinson Nuclear Plant strengthens its operational foundation, its financial challenges could impact long-term growth. Investors are encouraged to explore further insights about Duke Energy and its current market position.

Recent News 2 of 3, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 271 words

Wed Apr 22, 2026: Duke Energy Honored with Arbor Day Foundation’s Tree Line USA Award for 20th Year

Duke Energy Corporation has received national recognition for its tree care practices for the 20th consecutive year. The Arbor Day Foundation named Duke Energy Florida a 'Tree Line USA' utility, citing the company's rigorous tree pruning standards, comprehensive employee training, and public education efforts aimed at reducing power outages and safeguarding electrical infrastructure.

On 22nd April 2026, the Arbor Day Foundation lauded Duke Energy for meeting industry standards for tree care, supporting tree planting initiatives, and engaging in annual arbor education programs. The designation reflects Duke Energy's commitment to sustainable practices, as well as its proactive approach to maintaining the health of urban trees in proximity to electrical lines.

One of Duke Energy's ongoing initiatives includes an annual free tree giveaway for customers through the Arbor Day Foundation's Energy-Saving Trees program. In 2026, the company distributed 1,000 trees to Florida customers, bringing the total number of free trees given away statewide to over 16,000 since 2017. These trees are shipped directly to customers with detailed planting instructions designed to encourage proper placement away from power lines.

Company officials noted that proper tree placement is essential for conserving energy and minimizing the risk of outages during storm and hurricane seasons. Duke Energy also conducts year-round vegetation maintenance near power lines and ramps up these efforts ahead of hurricane season, ensuring a reliable power supply for its customers.

Additionally, Duke Energy reminds customers not to trim trees near power lines themselves and encourages them to contact Sunshine 811 before digging near underground utilities. More information about responsible tree planting near electrical equipment can be found through Duke Energy's Right Tree Right Place program.

Recent News 3 of 3, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 395 words

Wed Apr 22, 2026: Duke Energy Foundation Launches America250 Grants for South Carolina Green Spaces

GREENVILLE, S.C.– To strengthen communities across South Carolina as the nation approaches its 250th anniversary, Duke Energy Foundation is awarding $275,000 to nonprofit organizations through a first round of funding as part of its America250 initiative. This funding, announced on 22nd April 2026, celebrates Earth Day and aims to enhance local parks, tree planting efforts, and trail maintenance projects throughout the Palmetto State.

The grants, which form part of a total investment exceeding $550,000 in South Carolina, are aimed at revitalizing parks and shared green spaces, supporting tree planting, and litter prevention initiatives. "As we look back on our country's 250-year anniversary, it's an opportunity to reflect on our nation's history while working to support the next generation," said Tim Pearson, Duke Energy South Carolina president. "We are proud to partner with local organizations who can help care for the Palmetto State now, so future generations can enjoy its beauty."

Duke Energy Foundation has awarded a total of 11 grants in this first round of funding. Notable recipients include the South Carolina Wildlife Federation, Foothills Trail Conservancy, and the Upstate Greenways & Trails Alliance. Each organization is expected to utilize the funding to enhance local environments and foster community engagement in conservation efforts. For instance, Upstate Greenways & Trails Alliance plans to create a new trail that highlights the scenic beauty of Lake Keowee while educating visitors about the Revolutionary War history in the area.

The positive responses from grant recipients highlight the importance of such initiatives. Matt Flege, Executive Director of Upstate Greenways & Trails Alliance, expressed gratitude for the support, stating, "The America250 grant will allow us to create a new trail showcasing the beauty of Lake Keowee to South Carolina residents and visitors alike." Similarly, Susan H. Wild, Chair of Sumter Green, emphasized how the investment strengthens their commitment to beautifying the community, ensuring that efforts made today will benefit future generations.

These grants mark the first awards under Duke Energy Foundation's America250 initiative, which represents a commitment of over $1 million towards community-driven projects across the company's six-state service territories. Additional grant recipients will be announced later this spring, including initiatives focused on veterans' workforce pathways and enhancing access to history and civics education. Duke Energy Foundation continues to provide nearly $30 million annually in philanthropic support to the communities it serves, reflecting its dedication to environmental stewardship and community development.

SECTION 2 TODAY'S BULLISH SIGNALS

2.1 Uptrend

Today's Volatility:

The 5% discount to 12-month high of $134.49 against the 19% premium to 12-month low of $107.42 suggests the stock continues to push for new highs.

Price/Moving Average Price of 1.04:

- The Price/MAP 200 for Duke Energy Corporation is 1.04. Being higher than 1 is a bullish indicator. The stock is trading above both its MAPs and the 50-day MAP of $133.05 is higher than the 200-day MAP of $122.54, a second bullish indicator. The 200-day MAP has increased to $122.54, a third bullish indicator.

Past Quarter:

- In the last three months the stock has hit a new 52-week high twelve times, pointing to a significant uptrend.

2.2 Other Bullish Signals

- Return on Equity of 9.0% versus sector average of 5.8%.

MCap/Total Assets:

- Tobin's Q Ratio, defined as MCap divided by Total Assets, is 0.5. Compared with the rest of the market the stock is undervalued.

- Net profit margin has averaged 14.4% in the last 3 years. This is better than the sector average of 11.8% and suggests a high margin of safety.

- As per the Du Pont analysis, Return on Equity of 9.0% is better than sector average of 5.8%. This is computed as net profit margin of 15.2% times asset turnover [sales/assets] of 0.16 times leverage factor [total assets/shareholders' equity] of 3.6.

SECTION 3 ONGOING BULLISH PARAMETERS

3.1 Rule of 40

The stock scores a favorable score exceeding 40 when using the Rule of 40 (Revenue Growth plus EBITDA margin). Y.o.y revenue growth of 4%, EBITDA margin is 36.2% and the sum of the two 40.7% needs to exceed 40%.

Source: Duke Energy Corporation's, 10-K filing dated February 27, 2025, available at the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission website: https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/17797/000132616025000072/duk-20241231.htm and Duke Energy Corporation's website: https://investors.duke-energy.com/overview/default.aspx. company (Duke Energy Corporation), Ticker(DUK)

3.2 Created Market Value [CMV] past 17 yrs of $78.6 billion

- Market Capitalization has increased by $80.7 billion from $18.4 billion to $99.1 billion in the last 17 years. This increase comprises cumulative retained earnings (RETE) of $2 billion and Created Market Value of $78.6 billion. The Created Market Value multiple, defined by the change in MCap for every $1 of retained earnings is exemplary at $39.68.

3.3 Satisfies four criteria of Benjamin Graham

- The P/E of 22.3 is 0.4 times the highest average P/E of 57.7 in the last five years. This is a value criterion, according to Benjamin Graham who described as a value criterion "A P/E ratio down to less than four-tenth of the highest average P/E ratio the stock attained in the most recent five years".

-""Earnings growth over the most recent ten years of 7% compounded - that is a doubling of earnings in a five-year period""; the earnings per share have more than doubled in the last 5 years.

-""Stability of growth in earnings over the last 5 years, defined as no more than two declines of 5% or greater in year-end earnings""; there have been 2 declines in earnings in the last 5 years.

Source: Duke Energy Corporation's, 10-K filing dated February 27, 2025, available at the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission website: https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/17797/000132616025000072/duk-20241231.htm and Duke Energy Corporation's website: https://investors.duke-energy.com/overview/default.aspx. company (Duke Energy Corporation), Ticker(DUK)

3.4 Satisfies 5 out of 9 criteria of Joseph Piotroski [pass mark 5]:

- Positive net income.

- Positive operating cashflow.

- Return on Assets improvement [from 2.4% to 2.5%].

- Good quality of earnings [operating cashflow exceeds net income].

- Improvement in gross margin [from 98.0% to 98.1%].

But does not meet the following 4 criteria of Joseph Piotroski:

- Improvement in long-term debt to total assets.

- Improvement in current ratio.

- Total shares on issue unchanged (or reduction in total shares on issue).

- Improvement in asset turnover.

Source: Duke Energy Corporation's, 10-K filing dated February 27, 2025, available at the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission website: https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/17797/000132616025000072/duk-20241231.htm and Duke Energy Corporation's website: https://investors.duke-energy.com/overview/default.aspx. company (Duke Energy Corporation), Ticker(DUK)

5.1 LONG-TERM FUNDAMENTAL RANKING: 3 OUT OF 5 [5 is best]

Duke Energy Corporation is ranked number 1 out of 32 listed utility companies in the United States with a market capitalization of $99.1 billion.

Within its sector it has a relatively low P/E of 22.3 and low P/E/G of 0.36.

It has a moderate relative ROE of 9.0% and ROA of 2.5%. The company paid a dividend of $3.19 in the last twelve months. The dividend yield is moderate at 3.3%. Finally, its earnings growth in the past 12 months has been a comparatively high 61.3%.

Fri Apr 24, 2026: Duke Energy Secures 20-Year License for Robinson Nuclear Plant

Duke Energy Corporation has received a significant boost with the U.S. Nuclear Regulatory Commission's (NRC) decision to grant a 20-year extension for the operating license of its Robinson Nuclear Plant. This renewal allows the facility to continue producing energy until the year 2050, a crucial development for the utility giant. The announcement was made on April 23rd, 2026, and signifies Duke Energy's commitment to providing stable and sustainable energy to the region.

The Robinson Nuclear Plant, located in Hartsville, South Carolina, plays a vital role in Duke Energy's operations, supplying electricity to approximately 570,000 households. This license extension not only secures the plant's operational future but also aligns with Duke Energy's broader strategy of maintaining a diverse energy portfolio. The company is focused on integrating nuclear, natural gas, and renewable energy sources to meet the growing energy demands of its 8 million customers across various states, including the Carolinas, Indiana, Florida, Ohio, and Kentucky.

Duke Energy operates within the regulated utilities sector, boasting a market capitalization of approximately $99.64 billion. The company is segmented into electric utilities and infrastructure, gas utilities and infrastructure, and commercial renewables. Currently, the company's P/E ratio stands at 20.26, which is close to its five-year median, indicating that the stock may be fairly valued in the current market context. Investors are encouraged to consider this valuation alongside other financial metrics when evaluating the stock's potential.

The GF Score(TradeMark) for Duke Energy is 77 out of 100, reflecting strong performance across key metrics that can lead to higher long-term returns. The company's profitability rank is notably strong at 7 out of 10, underscoring its operational efficiency. However, concerns regarding financial strength persist, with a rating of just 3 out of 10, suggesting potential issues related to debt levels and overall financial health. Investors should carefully weigh these factors when considering Duke Energy as part of their investment portfolio.

In recent months, insider trading activity has raised eyebrows, with insiders selling $5.6 million worth of shares and no recorded insider buying. This trend may lead to questions about insider confidence in the company's future performance. Overall, Duke Energy presents a mixed outlook for investors. While the recent license renewal for the Robinson Nuclear Plant strengthens its operational foundation, its financial challenges could impact long-term growth. Investors are encouraged to explore further insights about Duke Energy and its current market position.

Recent News 2 of 3, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 271 words

Wed Apr 22, 2026: Duke Energy Honored with Arbor Day Foundation’s Tree Line USA Award for 20th Year

Duke Energy Corporation has received national recognition for its tree care practices for the 20th consecutive year. The Arbor Day Foundation named Duke Energy Florida a 'Tree Line USA' utility, citing the company's rigorous tree pruning standards, comprehensive employee training, and public education efforts aimed at reducing power outages and safeguarding electrical infrastructure.

On 22nd April 2026, the Arbor Day Foundation lauded Duke Energy for meeting industry standards for tree care, supporting tree planting initiatives, and engaging in annual arbor education programs. The designation reflects Duke Energy's commitment to sustainable practices, as well as its proactive approach to maintaining the health of urban trees in proximity to electrical lines.

One of Duke Energy's ongoing initiatives includes an annual free tree giveaway for customers through the Arbor Day Foundation's Energy-Saving Trees program. In 2026, the company distributed 1,000 trees to Florida customers, bringing the total number of free trees given away statewide to over 16,000 since 2017. These trees are shipped directly to customers with detailed planting instructions designed to encourage proper placement away from power lines.

Company officials noted that proper tree placement is essential for conserving energy and minimizing the risk of outages during storm and hurricane seasons. Duke Energy also conducts year-round vegetation maintenance near power lines and ramps up these efforts ahead of hurricane season, ensuring a reliable power supply for its customers.

Additionally, Duke Energy reminds customers not to trim trees near power lines themselves and encourages them to contact Sunshine 811 before digging near underground utilities. More information about responsible tree planting near electrical equipment can be found through Duke Energy's Right Tree Right Place program.

Recent News 3 of 3, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 395 words

Wed Apr 22, 2026: Duke Energy Foundation Launches America250 Grants for South Carolina Green Spaces

GREENVILLE, S.C.– To strengthen communities across South Carolina as the nation approaches its 250th anniversary, Duke Energy Foundation is awarding $275,000 to nonprofit organizations through a first round of funding as part of its America250 initiative. This funding, announced on 22nd April 2026, celebrates Earth Day and aims to enhance local parks, tree planting efforts, and trail maintenance projects throughout the Palmetto State.

The grants, which form part of a total investment exceeding $550,000 in South Carolina, are aimed at revitalizing parks and shared green spaces, supporting tree planting, and litter prevention initiatives. "As we look back on our country's 250-year anniversary, it's an opportunity to reflect on our nation's history while working to support the next generation," said Tim Pearson, Duke Energy South Carolina president. "We are proud to partner with local organizations who can help care for the Palmetto State now, so future generations can enjoy its beauty."

Duke Energy Foundation has awarded a total of 11 grants in this first round of funding. Notable recipients include the South Carolina Wildlife Federation, Foothills Trail Conservancy, and the Upstate Greenways & Trails Alliance. Each organization is expected to utilize the funding to enhance local environments and foster community engagement in conservation efforts. For instance, Upstate Greenways & Trails Alliance plans to create a new trail that highlights the scenic beauty of Lake Keowee while educating visitors about the Revolutionary War history in the area.

The positive responses from grant recipients highlight the importance of such initiatives. Matt Flege, Executive Director of Upstate Greenways & Trails Alliance, expressed gratitude for the support, stating, "The America250 grant will allow us to create a new trail showcasing the beauty of Lake Keowee to South Carolina residents and visitors alike." Similarly, Susan H. Wild, Chair of Sumter Green, emphasized how the investment strengthens their commitment to beautifying the community, ensuring that efforts made today will benefit future generations.

These grants mark the first awards under Duke Energy Foundation's America250 initiative, which represents a commitment of over $1 million towards community-driven projects across the company's six-state service territories. Additional grant recipients will be announced later this spring, including initiatives focused on veterans' workforce pathways and enhancing access to history and civics education. Duke Energy Foundation continues to provide nearly $30 million annually in philanthropic support to the communities it serves, reflecting its dedication to environmental stewardship and community development.

SECTION 2 TODAY'S BULLISH SIGNALS

2.1 Uptrend

Today's Volatility:

The 5% discount to 12-month high of $134.49 against the 19% premium to 12-month low of $107.42 suggests the stock continues to push for new highs.

Price/Moving Average Price of 1.04:

- The Price/MAP 200 for Duke Energy Corporation is 1.04. Being higher than 1 is a bullish indicator. The stock is trading above both its MAPs and the 50-day MAP of $133.04 is higher than the 200-day MAP of $122.60, a second bullish indicator. The 200-day MAP has increased to $122.60, a third bullish indicator.

Past Quarter:

- In the last three months the stock has hit a new 52-week high twelve times, pointing to a significant uptrend.

2.2 Other Bullish Signals

- Return on Equity of 9.0% versus sector average of 5.4%.

MCap/Total Assets:

- Tobin's Q Ratio, defined as MCap divided by Total Assets, is 0.5. Compared with the rest of the market the stock is undervalued.

- Net profit margin has averaged 14.4% in the last 3 years. This is better than the sector average of 11.8% and suggests a high margin of safety.

- As per the Du Pont analysis, Return on Equity of 9.0% is better than sector average of 5.4%. This is computed as net profit margin of 15.2% times asset turnover [sales/assets] of 0.16 times leverage factor [total assets/shareholders' equity] of 3.6.

SECTION 3 ONGOING BULLISH PARAMETERS

3.1 Rule of 40

The stock scores a favorable score exceeding 40 when using the Rule of 40 (Revenue Growth plus EBITDA margin). Y.o.y revenue growth of 4%, EBITDA margin is 36.2% and the sum of the two 40.7% needs to exceed 40%.

Source: Duke Energy Corporation's, 10-K filing dated February 27, 2025, available at the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission website: https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/17797/000132616025000072/duk-20241231.htm and Duke Energy Corporation's website: https://investors.duke-energy.com/overview/default.aspx. company (Duke Energy Corporation), Ticker(DUK)

3.2 Created Market Value [CMV] past 17 yrs of $78.3 billion

- Market Capitalization has increased by $80.4 billion from $18.8 billion to $99.2 billion in the last 17 years. This increase comprises cumulative retained earnings (RETE) of $2 billion and Created Market Value of $78.3 billion. The Created Market Value multiple, defined by the change in MCap for every $1 of retained earnings is exemplary at $39.53.

3.3 Satisfies four criteria of Benjamin Graham

- The P/E of 22.4 is 0.4 times the highest average P/E of 57.7 in the last five years. This is a value criterion, according to Benjamin Graham who described as a value criterion "A P/E ratio down to less than four-tenth of the highest average P/E ratio the stock attained in the most recent five years".

-""Earnings growth over the most recent ten years of 7% compounded - that is a doubling of earnings in a five-year period""; the earnings per share have more than doubled in the last 5 years.

-""Stability of growth in earnings over the last 5 years, defined as no more than two declines of 5% or greater in year-end earnings""; there have been 2 declines in earnings in the last 5 years.

Source: Duke Energy Corporation's, 10-K filing dated February 27, 2025, available at the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission website: https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/17797/000132616025000072/duk-20241231.htm and Duke Energy Corporation's website: https://investors.duke-energy.com/overview/default.aspx. company (Duke Energy Corporation), Ticker(DUK)

3.4 Satisfies 5 out of 9 criteria of Joseph Piotroski [pass mark 5]:

- Positive net income.

- Positive operating cashflow.

- Return on Assets improvement [from 2.4% to 2.5%].

- Good quality of earnings [operating cashflow exceeds net income].

- Improvement in gross margin [from 98.0% to 98.1%].

But does not meet the following 4 criteria of Joseph Piotroski:

- Improvement in long-term debt to total assets.

- Improvement in current ratio.

- Total shares on issue unchanged (or reduction in total shares on issue).

- Improvement in asset turnover.

It has a moderate relative ROE of 9.0% and ROA of 2.5%. The company paid a dividend of $3.19 in the last twelve months. The dividend yield is moderate at 3.3%. Finally, its earnings growth in the past 12 months has been a comparatively high 61.3%.

urn:contentItem:6JHC-G413-RT9J-205N-00000-00DENTONS US LLP - INDUSTRY HIGHLIGHTS INCLUDING PEERS2026-05-06T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:11:22ZLexisNexisSTOCKDENTONS US LLP - INDUSTRY HIGHLIGHTS INCLUDING PEERS

DASHBOARD: DENTONS US LLP

PermID4297902658Websitehttp://www.dentons.comIndustryFull-Service - Global

ACTIVITIES:

Dentons US LLP, part of Dentons, the worlds largest law firm, was founded in 2013 through a merger and is headquartered in Washington, D.C. With over 1,000 U.S. lawyers across 20 offices, it serves clients in technology, energy, and healthcare, specializing in corporate law, litigation, and IP. In 2024, Dentons advised on cross-border M&As and expanded its startup practice, per its recognition among top firms for SaaS companies. Its culture emphasizes global collaboration, diversity, and pro bono work. Competing with DLA Piper, Dentons stands out for its polycentric model and extensive global network. Recent initiatives include digital transformation and ESG-focused legal services.

+ DENTONS US LLP [3 Press Releases]

+ REAL ESTATE TEAM EXPANDS DENTONS US' CHARLESTON, SOUTH CAROLINA, OFFICE

Dentons US announced today the continued expansion of its Charleston, South Carolina, office with the addition of Real Estate partners Ryan Goer and Bryan Walpole. They are joined by Real Estate counsel Steve McCrae and Caitlin Nevin, further strengthening the Firm's growing presence in the region.

The additions come just one week after Dentons US announced the opening of its Charleston office, marking another step forward in the Firm's strategic expansion into one of the fastest-growing markets in the country. The Firm sees Charleston as a key hub for supporting clients across an expanding business community, a strong regional talent pool, and industries critical to long-term economic growth.

"The pace at which we are building our Charleston office speaks to both the opportunity in this market and our confidence in its long-term potential," said Dentons US Chief Executive Officer Sonia Martin. "Real estate is a core practice for Dentons US and a key area of strategic investment. This continued expansion strengthens our ability to serve clients across the South and reinforces our commitment to strategic, sustainable growth."

Goer and Walpole advise mortgage portfolio lenders, servicers, borrowers, and private and institutional lenders on a wide range of secured lending matters. Their experience includes loan origination and the servicing of securitized loans, including defeasances, assumptions, modifications, consents, and other servicing matters. Walpole also advises on the purchase and sale of distressed debt.

McCrae and Nevin represent clients across complex commercial real estate transactions and development matters. Nevin also advises on acquisitions, dispositions, financings, leasing, and joint ventures and has particular experience in renewable energy development, retail leasing, zoning, and entitlements, including South Carolina Fee‑in‑Lieu of Tax (FILOT) agreements. McCrae focuses on governmental entitlements and economic incentives for industrial and developer clients and is a LEED Accredited Professional.

"Building depth and capability in Charleston is a key priority for the Firm," said John Holahan, Managing Partner of Dentons US. "These additions reflect our commitment to growing a high-performing office in a rapidly growing market that pairs well nationally with the strength of our leading practice groups and presence across money centers."

The Charleston office opened with a group of eight partners across multiple practices. The initial Real Estate team included Amy Ironmonger, Bo Manuel, and Matt Norton. Walker Coleman and Cecilia Ehresman joined the Employment and Labor practice, while Jamie Bruce, Ernie Cochran, and Cooper Hawley joined the Corporate practice.

Source: Company Website

+ DENTONS WELCOMES BACK KAMIL UMAR AS A PARTNER IN THE MARKET-LEADING CALGARY REAL ESTATE GROUP

Dentons, Canada's Global Law Firm, is pleased to welcome Kamil Umar back to the Firm as a partner in the national Real Estate group. This addition reflects the Firm's continued investment in building a national real estate practice that anticipates the needs of clients across Canada as the sector evolves.

"The connections I maintained with Dentons colleagues over the years made the decision to return an easy one," said Kamil Umar. "The Firm's global reach, collaborative culture and depth across key sectors create a strong foundation for serving clients in Calgary, across Canada and the world. I'm looking forward to building on that momentum and contributing to what is already a strong and growing real estate practice."

Kamil Umar brings a business-minded, market-driven approach to complex commercial real estate matters, with a strong track record advising on some of Calgary's most significant public infrastructure and development projects. His practice spans property acquisitions and dispositions, land development and land use planning, condominium and strata development, leasing and financing, as well as public infrastructure work. He advises a diverse range of clients-from residential and commercial developers to pension funds and institutional investors-providing practical, commercially grounded guidance.

"Real estate is one of the most dynamic sectors in Canada right now," said Clark Kassian, National Leader of the Real Estate group. "Kamil is an exceptional lawyer who brings the calibre of expertise and market insight clients are looking for as they pursue their most ambitious goals. He is exactly the kind of talent we seek as we continue strengthening our leading real estate group."

Calgary Managing Partner, John Cusano, shared, "We are delighted to welcome Kamil back to Dentons. His return reflects the strength of the team we have built here. Having previously developed his practice at the Firm, Kamil is widely recognized in the Calgary market, and his track record advising on some of the province's most significant projects positions him exceptionally well to support clients as real estate activity continues to evolve."

Dentons Canada's Real Estate group is recognized for its ability to advise on all aspects of real estate transactions, development and financing. Our team provides strategic counsel to developers, investors, owners, landlords, tenants and lenders on acquisitions and dispositions, condominium development and sales, commercial leasing, municipal and property development, financing and restructuring, joint ventures and partnerships, complex land assemblies, and hospitality-related matters, including hotel acquisitions and dispositions, management and franchise agreements, development projects and operational considerations, across Canada and internationally.

Source: Company Website

+ DENTONS CANADA ADVISES ENBRIDGE ON FEDERAL APPROVAL OF $4B SUNRISE EXPANSION PROGRAM IN BRITISH COLUMBIA

Dentons, Canada's Global Law Firm, is pleased to have advised Enbridge Inc. in connection with the Government of Canada's approval of the Sunrise Expansion Program, a CA$4 billion natural gas pipeline expansion in British Columbia.

The Sunrise Expansion Program will expand Enbridge's Westcoast pipeline system through the addition of pipeline looping, increased compression and related facility upgrades. The project is expected to provide approximately 300 million cubic feet per day of additional natural gas transportation capacity, supporting growing energy demand across the province.

This step represents a significant milestone for this major energy infrastructure project that is expected to enhance energy security, support industrial development and LNG export capacity, as well as contribute to broader economic growth in Canada. Construction is anticipated to begin in 2026.

Dentons acted for Enbridge on its application to the Canada Energy Regulator in connection with the expansion of a portion of its pipeline system. The legal team was led by Laura Estep, with support from Dan Collins.

"We are proud to have supported Enbridge on this important initiative, which will play a key role in meeting Canada's growing energy needs," said Laura Estep, partner in the Energy Regulatory group.

Source: Company Website

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ K&L GATES [3 Press Releases]

+ TWO K&L GATES PARTNERS AGAIN NAMED TO INDIA BUSINESS LAW JOURNALS INTERNATIONAL A-LIST 2026

K&L Gates is pleased to announce that Singapore Office Managing Partner Raja Bose and Singapore Corporate partner Meraj Noor have once again been named to India Business Law Journal's (IBLJ) International A-List. This marks the eighth consecutive year both partners have received this recognition.

IBLJ's International A-List identifies the top lawyers based outside of India who are recognized for their work on India-related matters. The A-List was compiled by IBLJ's seasoned editorial team following extensive market research, drawing on feedback from hundreds of lawyers at Indian law firms and thousands of India-focused in-house counsel within India and abroad.

The full International A-List 2026 is featured on IBLJ's website.

**All Singapore lawyers are members of K&L Gates Straits Law LLC, the Singapore office of global law firm K&L Gates. Source: Company Website

+ MORE THAN 80 K&L GATES LAWYERS ACROSS ASIA-PACIFIC RECOGNISED IN 2027 BEST LAWYERS, ONES TO WATCH EDITIONS

Best Lawyers®, a peer-review publication in the legal profession, has named more than 80 K&L Gates lawyers across the Asia-Pacific region in the 2027 editions of its guides, covering Australia, Japan, and Singapore. In addition, nearly 20 of the firm's lawyers were named as Best Lawyers: Ones to Watch® in Australia and Japan, lists that recognise lawyers early in their careers.

Alongside these recognitions, four Australian partners were recognised with "Lawyer of the Year" awards, an accolade that is particularly noteworthy, as it is only bestowed on one standout lawyer for each practice area in each jurisdiction.

The Best Lawyers® guides highlight top legal talent, selected by peers within the same geographical region and legal practice area, through millions of votes each year. Congratulations to the Lawyer of the Year award recipients below, as well as all lawyers recognised in the 2027 editions - resulting in nearly 180 total recognitions for the firm. Source: Company Website

+ K&L GATES ADVISES DOLPHIN ENTERTAINMENT, INC. ON US$15 MILLION PIPE INVESTMENT

Global law firm K&L Gates LLP advised Dolphin Entertainment, Inc., an entertainment marketing and premium content production company, in connection with a US$15 million private investment in public equity (PIPE) financing with Lincoln Park Capital, an investment group and asset management firm.

The Corporate team advising on the transaction was led by Miami partner Clayton Parker with support from Miami partner Erin Fogarty.

"This transaction underscores Dolphin Entertainment's commitment to accelerating strategic growth across its marketing and content-driven businesses. We were pleased to support them in securing investment from a partner aligned with its long-term vision," said Parker.

K&L Gates' Corporate practice is one of the most substantial in the legal industry, with hundreds of lawyers in offices across the globe providing clients with practical legal solutions in the structuring, financing, and completion of domestic, international, and cross-border transactions. Source: Company Website

Global law firm White & Case LLP has advised NRG Energy, Inc. (NYSE: NRG), a leading North American energy and home services company, on its offering of US$500 million of senior secured first-lien notes and US$2.1 billion of senior unsecured notes, and its new US$900 million term loan B facility.

(Beretta Holding) with Ruger's 2026 annual meeting. Further information is available on Ruger's Form 8-K (available here) and in the press release (available here).

Source: Company Website

+ WHITE & CASE ADVISES HEIRS OF MARTHA NATHAN PRO BONO ON RECOVERY OF PAINTING BY FERDINAND HODLER

Global law firm White & Case LLP has advised certain heirs of Martha Adrianna Nathan, born Dreyfus (1874 - 1958), on proceedings before the Independent Commission SKKG ("the Commission") concerning the painting "Thunersee mit Blu u mlisalp und Niesen" (1882) by Ferdinand Hodler, which has been in the Stiftung fur Kunst, Kultur und Geschichte (SKKG) collection since 1998.

+ JONES DAY [3 Press Releases]

+ FORMER SEC DEPUTY DIRECTOR NEKIA HACKWORTH JOINS JONES DAY AS PARTNER IN ATLANTA

Nekia Hackworth has joined Jones Day as a partner in its Investigations & White Collar Defense Practice. She is based in the Firm's Atlanta Office.

Ms. Hackworth has more than 20 years of experience in both government and private practice as a lawyer representing clients in white-collar criminal cases and civil litigation, conducting government and internal investigations, and counseling companies on corporate compliance matters. She served most recently as the Deputy Director of Enforcement in the Southeast Region for the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC), where she supervised more than 250 professionals with responsibility for SEC matters across three offices (Washington, Atlanta, and Miami) and 11 jurisdictions. She oversaw hundreds of investigations in her tenure at the SEC, including suspected market manipulation, insider trading, and investment adviser fraud.

Source: Company Website

+ FORMER SEC DEPUTY DIRECTOR NEKIA HACKWORTH JOINS JONES DAY AS PARTNER IN ATLANTA

Nekia Hackworth has joined Jones Day as a partner in its Investigations & White Collar Defense Practice. She is based in the Firm's Atlanta Office.

Ms. Hackworth has more than 20 years of experience in both government and private practice as a lawyer representing clients in white-collar criminal cases and civil litigation, conducting government and internal investigations, and counseling companies on corporate compliance matters. She served most recently as the Deputy Director of Enforcement in the Southeast Region for the U.S. Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC), where she supervised more than 250 professionals with responsibility for SEC matters across three offices (Washington, Atlanta, and Miami) and 11 jurisdictions. She oversaw hundreds of investigations in her tenure at the SEC, including suspected market manipulation, insider trading, and investment adviser fraud.

Source: Company Website

+ DERIVATIVES LAWYER COLIN RICE JOINS JONES DAY IN SINGAPORE

Colin Rice has joined Jones Day as a partner in its Financial Markets Practice. He is based in the Firm's Singapore Office.

Mr. Rice is an experienced derivatives lawyer who has counseled clients on a broad range of financial markets transactions, with a particular focus on OTC derivatives, structured products, structured finance transactions, repos, and securities lending. He works with global investment banks, retail banks, funds, corporates, and service providers. He also advises on uncleared derivatives margin reform and collateral arrangements, default management, and the impact of global regulatory developments on derivatives businesses and documentation.

"Colin is an important addition to our global derivatives team. Asia is a fast-growing market with more and more netting friendly jurisdictions, and he is among the most experienced lawyers in the region on these matters," said Alban Caillemer du Ferrage, chair of Jones Day's Financial Markets department for EMEA and APAC. "Colin served as a director in the Singapore office of one of the world's largest investment banks. We will be able to work with our largest clients on their most complex derivatives transactions there. I am delighted to welcome him to our global group."

Source: Company Website

+ GREENBERG TRAURIG ATTORNEYS RECOGNIZED ON 2026 VIRGINIA SUPER LAWYERS 'RISING STARS LIST

McLEAN, Va. - April 28, 2026 - Three attorneys from global law firm Greenberg Traurig, LLP were selected for inclusion on the 2026 Virginia Super Lawyers "Rising Stars" list, recognizing outstanding young lawyers across a range of practice areas.

Super Lawyers evaluates attorneys statewide across more than 70 practice areas. The "Rising Stars" designation is awarded to no more than 2.5% of lawyers practicing in each state who demonstrate outstanding professional achievement and peer recognition early in their careers. Candidates are identified through a rigorous selection process that combines peer nominations with independent research and evaluation.

+ GREENBERG TRAURIG TOKYO HOSTS ANREV BRIEFING ON REAL ASSETS, DATA CENTRES AND AI

TOKYO - April 30, 2026 - Greenberg Traurig, LLP's Tokyo office recently hosted and sponsored a briefing for the Asian Association for Investors in Non‑Listed Real Estate Vehicles (ANREV), bringing together institutional investors and industry professionals to discuss emerging trends in real assets, data centres, and artificial intelligence.

The program featured a panel discussion titled "Real Assets & New Technologies: Data Centres & AI Opportunities," with Greenberg Traurig Shareholder Joel H. Rothstein, chair of the firm's Asia Real Estate Practice, among the speakers. Panelists discussed how advances in AI are reshaping capital allocation, infrastructure planning, and investment strategies across global real asset markets, with a particular focus on the growing demand for data centre assets throughout the Asia‑Pacific region.

Source: Company Website

+ BAKER MCKENZIE WELCOMES RENOWNED IP AND TECHNOLOGY PARTNER CONSTANTIN REHAAG IN FRANKFURT

Global law firm Baker McKenzie is further strengthening its global Commercial Practice Group with the addition of Dr. iur. Constantin Rehaag, M.A., a renowned expert who will be based in Frankfurt. His arrival significantly enhances the firm's cross-border advisory capabilities in Intellectual Property (IP) and Technology across EMEA. + NORTON ROSE FULBRIGHT [4 Press Releases]

+ NORTON ROSE FULBRIGHT ADVISES LOUIS DREYFUS COMPANY ON ITS NEW EUR 3.5 BILLION GUARANTEED EURO MEDIUM TERM NOTE PROGRAMME AND THE DEBUT NOTES ISSUANCE THEREUNDER

Global law firm Norton Rose Fulbright has advised Louis Dreyfus Company (LDC), a leading global merchant and processor of agricultural goods, on the establishment by Louis Dreyfus Company Finance B.V. of its EUR 3.5 billion Euro Medium Term Note (EMTN) Programme guaranteed by Louis Dreyfus Company B.V. and its debut issuance of EUR 500 million Notes thereunder.

The 7-year notes are sized at EUR 500 million, have a coupon of 4.0 percent per annum and are rated BBB+ by S&P Global Ratings. The Notes are listed on the Luxembourg Stock Exchange's regulated market.

Founded in 1851, LDC operates across a diverse range of business lines - with activities that span the entire value chain, from origination to distribution. The company employs over 20,000 people worldwide and is active in more than 100 countries.

LDC's EMTN programme has been established to support the Group's long-term funding strategy and enhance financial flexibility.

The cross-border Norton Rose Fulbright team advising LDC on this transaction was led by Paris-based capital markets partner Jeremy Grant, with assistance from London-based counsel Julian Walley and Paris-based associate Elise Najjar and jurist Charlotte Armagnac. NautaDutilh advised the issuer and guarantor as to Netherlands law. Clifford Chance advised the dealers.

Jeremy Grant commented: "We were proud to continue our longstanding relationship with Louis Dreyfus Company by supporting the Group on this important milestone. LDC was able to price its debut programme issuance under excellent conditions despite volatile markets."

Source: Company Website

+ NORTON ROSE FULBRIGHT ADVISES MAB ON MAIN MARKET LISTING

Global law firm Norton Rose Fulbright has advised Mortgage Advice Bureau (Holdings) plc (MAB) on its move from AIM to the Equity Shares (Commercial Companies) category of the Official List of the Financial Conduct Authority (FCA) and to trading on the Main Market of the London Stock Exchange.

MAB is a leading FCA-regulated UK property finance platform that connects customers, advisers, lenders, and insurers throughout the homeownership journey. Through its scalable, technology-driven intermediary model, MAB delivers personalised mortgage and protection advice via its proprietary platform, supported by customer insight and a data-driven, digitally enabled framework.

Norton Rose Fulbright initially advised MAB on its AIM IPO in 2014 and has continued to support the company since that time, including advising on its acquisition of Fluent Money and associated equity fundraising in 2022.

The Norton Rose Fulbright team was led by corporate partners Richard Sheen and Fiona Millington, and counsel Alex Green. They were supported by consultant Deborah Wilcher, associate Zainab Hamid and trainee solicitor Isabella Green (corporate); partner Matthew Gregory (financial services regulatory), partner Paul Griffin and counsel Ben Wright (employment); partner Matthew Findley and associate Katharine Wadia (employee benefits and executive compensation); and partner Michael Alliston and senior associate Elisabeth Trotter (tax).

Source: Company Website

+ NORTON ROSE FULBRIGHT ADVISES QUINBROOK ON UK'S LARGEST SYNCHRONOUS CONDENSER PORTFOLIO FINANCING

Global law firm Norton Rose Fulbright has advised Quinbrook Infrastructure Partners (Quinbrook) on a landmark £156 million debt financing for the UK's largest synchronous condenser portfolio.

The financing supports Quinbrook's Severn portfolio of synchronous condensers, comprising of the Cilfynydd project in South Wales and the Sellindge project in Kent. The transaction was led by a consortium of lenders including Standard Chartered, Barclays and Siemens Financial Services through Siemens Bank.

Both projects are currently under construction and are expected to be completed in 2027 and 2028 respectively. Once operational, the Severn portfolio is expected to deliver a combined 2,531 MVA of short‑circuit level, providing essential stability services to the UK electricity grid.

The multidisciplinary Norton Rose Fulbright team was led by London‑based Global Co-Head of Energy Rob Marsh, who was supported by special counsel Chris Aird, associates Georgia Foulkes‑Hartley and Hannah Duke and a wider team on the finance and due diligence work streams.

Rob Marsh commented:

"It has been a pleasure to support Quinbrook on another successful financing in this sector and to continue our partnership with the team. Synchronous condensers will play an increasingly important role in maintaining grid stability and resilience and Quinbrook are now the largest private owners of this asset class in the UK so we are delighted to have helped them deliver this second portfolio financing."

Norton Rose Fulbright recently advised Quinbrook on the UK's largest PV and battery storage project financing for Cleve Hill Solar Park as well as Quinbrook's first investment in Ireland.

Source: Company Website

+ NORTON ROSE FULBRIGHT ADVISES THE EUROPEAN BANK FOR RECONSTRUCTION AND DEVELOPMENT ON EUR180M FINANCING FOR LPP

Global law firm Norton Rose Fulbright has advised the European Bank for Reconstruction and Development (EBRD) on the EUR180m financing for LPP, a Polish family-owned fashion group (the Group) operating five brands across 46 markets worldwide.

The financing covered a sustainability-linked loan agreement, divided into committed and uncommitted tranches. The financing is intended primarily for cross-border expansion by the Group of the retail space in Ukraine and corporate investment purposes, both in line with LMA Sustainability-Linked Loan Principles.

The Norton Rose Fulbright team in Warsaw was led by counsel Jacek Smardzewski and included associate Michal Rutkowski working under the supervision of partner Grzegorz Dyczkowski, who heads the firm's Warsaw office and Warsaw banking and finance practice. London-based Norton Rose Fulbright partner James Dunnett also advised on this transaction.

Final Milestone acted as exclusive financial adviser to LPP, while White & Case served as LPP's legal adviser.

Source: Company Website

+ MORGAN LEWIS & BOCKIUS LLP [3 Press Releases]

+ MORGAN LEWIS & BOCKIUS LLP EU PAY TRANSPARENCY DIRECTIVE: THE COUNTDOWN IS ON, HR EXECUTIVE

Partner Louise Skinner and associate Margaret McDowell were quoted by HR Executive in an article examining the approaching compliance deadline for the European Union's Pay Transparency Directive and its implications for multinational employers.

Louise emphasized the need for employers to proactively assess hiring practices and prepare for increased transparency, noting that "multinationals with European operations should assess whether current recruitment processes are compliant with the new requirements that apply during the hiring process."

Margaret highlighted compliance challenges arising from varying US requirements, explaining that "some of the most consequential differences that we have seen be overlooked include which roles are covered. when the disclosure obligation is triggered and what information must be disclosed," and cautioning that "the cost of getting this wrong can be significant."

Source: Company Website

+ INTERNATIONAL EMPLOYMENT LAWYER NAMES MORGAN LEWIS'S LAKEISHA ANDRESS AMONG 2026 TOMORROW'S LEADERS

DALLAS, April 28, 2026: Morgan Lewis of counsel Lakeisha Andress has been named to the International Employment Lawyer list of 2026 Tomorrow's Leaders. The annual program highlights legal professionals from around the world who have been involved in high-profile or innovative disputes, projects, or deals during their first decade of practice.

Lakeisha represents employers in a wide range of complex employment matters, including sensitive and high-profile internal investigations, strategic counseling, and litigation. She conducts investigations into allegations of workplace misconduct at all company levels, such as discrimination, sexual harassment, hostile work environment, and whistleblower claims. She also strategically advises employers on all aspects of the workplace.

Source: Company Website

+ MORGAN LEWIS & BOCKIUS LLP FTC STEPS UP ENFORCEMENT OF "MADE IN USA" LABELING RULE, FTCWATCH

FTCWatch quoted associate Caiti Zeytoonian in an article examining the renewed enforcement efforts by the Federal Trade Commission (FTC) of its "Made in USA" Labeling Rule.

The article discusses a series of recent FTC complaints targeting companies accused of falsely marketing imported goods as US-made, signaling a more coordinated enforcement approach. Caiti noted that the agency's focus reflects both consumer protection priorities and the broader significance of origin claims in the marketplace.

"The agency wants to protect consumers and prioritize preserving the importance and the value of 'Made in USA' claims," Caiti said. "An American flag, an American flagpole, is the quintessential American product."

Source: Company Website

+ CLIFFORD CHANCE ADVISES FINANCIERS ON THE FINANCING OF THE LANDMARK MIRNY WIND PROJECT IN KAZAKHSTAN

Clifford Chance has advised the financiers - EBRD, Proparco, DEG, Development Bank of Kazakhstan, Societe Generale, QNB Group, China Construction Bank and Standard Chartered - in connection with the financing of Mirny project in Kazakhstan, developed by TotalEnergies, Samruk Energy and KazMunayGas.

+ CLIFFORD CHANCE ERNENNT WELTWEIT 28 NEUE PARTNERINNEN UND PARTNER, DREI DAVON IN DEUTSCHLAND

Die globale Anwaltssozietat Clifford Chance ernennt zum neuen Geschaftsjahr am 1. Mai 2026 weltweit 28 Rechtsanwaltinnen und Rechtsanwalte zu Partnerinnen und Partnern, darunter drei in Deutschland.

+ CLIFFORD CHANCE ADVISES BNP PARIBAS ON U.S.$120M SPEKBOOM RESTORATION-LINKED BOND

Global law firm Clifford Chance has advised BNP Paribas as arranger of a 14-year outcome bond issued by the International Bank for Reconstruction and Development ("IBRD"), part of the World Bank Group. Interest on the bond is linked to carbon removal units ("CRUs") issued by a project designed, developed and operated by Imperative, a private-company specializing in ecosystem restoration, and sold to Amazon.

urn:contentItem:6JH0-1DY3-RV6X-J37J-00000-00American Electric Power's dividend in perspective: comparison with Dominion Energy, Inc. *Updated*2026-05-04T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:07:21ZLexisNexisAmerican Electric Power's dividend in perspective: comparison with Dominion Energy, Inc. *Updated*

NASDAQ STOCK REPORT

American Electric Power's trailing twelve months dividend is $3.76. Its dividend yield at its current price is 2.74%. Its current price is at a 0.5% discount to its 52 week high. Its current price is at a 44.1% premium to its 52 week low. If American Electric Power was to return to its 52 week low its dividend yield would be 3.95%.

In comparison, Dominion Energy, Inc.'s trailing twelve months dividend is $2.67. Its dividend yield at its current price is 4.14%. Its current price is at a 3.5% discount to its 52 week high. Its current price is at a 28.5% premium to its 52 week low. If Dominion Energy, Inc. was to return to its 52 week low its dividend yield would be 5.32%.

$1000 invested into American Electric Power stock 5 years ago would be worth $2,176 today. This total gain of $1,176 is generated by a capital gain of $849 and dividends of $326.

$1000 invested into Dominion Energy, Inc. stock 5 years ago would be worth $1,243 today. This total gain of $243 is generated by a capital gain of $18 and dividends of $224.

American Electric Power's dividend has grown 5.3% over the last 4 years.

Dominion Energy, Inc.'s dividend has grown 0% over the last 4 years.

Company Profile

American Electric Power Company, Inc. (AEP) is a regulated electric utility serving approximately 5.6 million customers across 11 U.S. states. The company generates the largest portion of its revenue from retail electric sales within its Vertically Integrated Utilities and Transmission & Distribution Utilities segments. In 2025, these segments contributed significantly to the $21.88 billion in total revenues, with regulated rates on residential, commercial, and industrial loads forming the core. The fastest-growing area was commercial retail within Transmission & Distribution, which saw a 27.8% year-to-date increase, driven by demand from data centers and hyperscalers. Incremental transmission investments and wholesale activities further supported overall load expansion.

Company Profile

Dominion Energy operates primarily in regulated electricity and natural gas services, with its largest revenue sources stemming from electric operations in Virginia, North Carolina, and South Carolina, serving 3.6 million customers. Natural gas distribution in South Carolina, with 500,000 customers, also contributes significantly to revenue. In 2025, the company generated $16.5 billion in operating revenue, primarily from these segments. The fastest-growing sections include contracted energy, which saw a 22% year-over-year increase in operating earnings to $438 million, driven by investments in renewable energy projects such as offshore wind and solar. Additionally, Dominion is the largest producer of carbon-free electricity in New England, leveraging nuclear production to support its clean energy initiatives.

Fri Apr 24, 2026: Duke Energy Secures 20-Year License for Robinson Nuclear Plant

Duke Energy Corporation has received a significant boost with the U.S. Nuclear Regulatory Commission's (NRC) decision to grant a 20-year extension for the operating license of its Robinson Nuclear Plant. This renewal allows the facility to continue producing energy until the year 2050, a crucial development for the utility giant. The announcement was made on April 23rd, 2026, and signifies Duke Energy's commitment to providing stable and sustainable energy to the region.

The Robinson Nuclear Plant, located in Hartsville, South Carolina, plays a vital role in Duke Energy's operations, supplying electricity to approximately 570,000 households. This license extension not only secures the plant's operational future but also aligns with Duke Energy's broader strategy of maintaining a diverse energy portfolio. The company is focused on integrating nuclear, natural gas, and renewable energy sources to meet the growing energy demands of its 8 million customers across various states, including the Carolinas, Indiana, Florida, Ohio, and Kentucky.

Duke Energy operates within the regulated utilities sector, boasting a market capitalization of approximately $99.64 billion. The company is segmented into electric utilities and infrastructure, gas utilities and infrastructure, and commercial renewables. Currently, the company's P/E ratio stands at 20.26, which is close to its five-year median, indicating that the stock may be fairly valued in the current market context. Investors are encouraged to consider this valuation alongside other financial metrics when evaluating the stock's potential.

The GF Score(TradeMark) for Duke Energy is 77 out of 100, reflecting strong performance across key metrics that can lead to higher long-term returns. The company's profitability rank is notably strong at 7 out of 10, underscoring its operational efficiency. However, concerns regarding financial strength persist, with a rating of just 3 out of 10, suggesting potential issues related to debt levels and overall financial health. Investors should carefully weigh these factors when considering Duke Energy as part of their investment portfolio.

In recent months, insider trading activity has raised eyebrows, with insiders selling $5.6 million worth of shares and no recorded insider buying. This trend may lead to questions about insider confidence in the company's future performance. Overall, Duke Energy presents a mixed outlook for investors. While the recent license renewal for the Robinson Nuclear Plant strengthens its operational foundation, its financial challenges could impact long-term growth. Investors are encouraged to explore further insights about Duke Energy and its current market position.

Recent News 2 of 3, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 271 words

Wed Apr 22, 2026: Duke Energy Honored with Arbor Day Foundation’s Tree Line USA Award for 20th Year

Duke Energy Corporation has received national recognition for its tree care practices for the 20th consecutive year. The Arbor Day Foundation named Duke Energy Florida a 'Tree Line USA' utility, citing the company's rigorous tree pruning standards, comprehensive employee training, and public education efforts aimed at reducing power outages and safeguarding electrical infrastructure.

On 22nd April 2026, the Arbor Day Foundation lauded Duke Energy for meeting industry standards for tree care, supporting tree planting initiatives, and engaging in annual arbor education programs. The designation reflects Duke Energy's commitment to sustainable practices, as well as its proactive approach to maintaining the health of urban trees in proximity to electrical lines.

One of Duke Energy's ongoing initiatives includes an annual free tree giveaway for customers through the Arbor Day Foundation's Energy-Saving Trees program. In 2026, the company distributed 1,000 trees to Florida customers, bringing the total number of free trees given away statewide to over 16,000 since 2017. These trees are shipped directly to customers with detailed planting instructions designed to encourage proper placement away from power lines.

Company officials noted that proper tree placement is essential for conserving energy and minimizing the risk of outages during storm and hurricane seasons. Duke Energy also conducts year-round vegetation maintenance near power lines and ramps up these efforts ahead of hurricane season, ensuring a reliable power supply for its customers.

Additionally, Duke Energy reminds customers not to trim trees near power lines themselves and encourages them to contact Sunshine 811 before digging near underground utilities. More information about responsible tree planting near electrical equipment can be found through Duke Energy's Right Tree Right Place program.

Recent News 3 of 3, Source: HourlyBusinessNews, 395 words

Wed Apr 22, 2026: Duke Energy Foundation Launches America250 Grants for South Carolina Green Spaces

GREENVILLE, S.C.– To strengthen communities across South Carolina as the nation approaches its 250th anniversary, Duke Energy Foundation is awarding $275,000 to nonprofit organizations through a first round of funding as part of its America250 initiative. This funding, announced on 22nd April 2026, celebrates Earth Day and aims to enhance local parks, tree planting efforts, and trail maintenance projects throughout the Palmetto State.

The grants, which form part of a total investment exceeding $550,000 in South Carolina, are aimed at revitalizing parks and shared green spaces, supporting tree planting, and litter prevention initiatives. "As we look back on our country's 250-year anniversary, it's an opportunity to reflect on our nation's history while working to support the next generation," said Tim Pearson, Duke Energy South Carolina president. "We are proud to partner with local organizations who can help care for the Palmetto State now, so future generations can enjoy its beauty."

Duke Energy Foundation has awarded a total of 11 grants in this first round of funding. Notable recipients include the South Carolina Wildlife Federation, Foothills Trail Conservancy, and the Upstate Greenways & Trails Alliance. Each organization is expected to utilize the funding to enhance local environments and foster community engagement in conservation efforts. For instance, Upstate Greenways & Trails Alliance plans to create a new trail that highlights the scenic beauty of Lake Keowee while educating visitors about the Revolutionary War history in the area.

The positive responses from grant recipients highlight the importance of such initiatives. Matt Flege, Executive Director of Upstate Greenways & Trails Alliance, expressed gratitude for the support, stating, "The America250 grant will allow us to create a new trail showcasing the beauty of Lake Keowee to South Carolina residents and visitors alike." Similarly, Susan H. Wild, Chair of Sumter Green, emphasized how the investment strengthens their commitment to beautifying the community, ensuring that efforts made today will benefit future generations.

These grants mark the first awards under Duke Energy Foundation's America250 initiative, which represents a commitment of over $1 million towards community-driven projects across the company's six-state service territories. Additional grant recipients will be announced later this spring, including initiatives focused on veterans' workforce pathways and enhancing access to history and civics education. Duke Energy Foundation continues to provide nearly $30 million annually in philanthropic support to the communities it serves, reflecting its dedication to environmental stewardship and community development.

SECTION 2 TODAY'S BULLISH SIGNALS

2.1 Uptrend

Today's Volatility:

The 4% discount to 12-month high of $134.49 against the 20% premium to 12-month low of $107.42 suggests the stock continues to push for new highs.

Today its volatility (highest price minus lowest price/lowest price) of 1.6% equalled the average daily volatility, up from 1.2584091808468567% on Wednesday. Price/Moving Average Price of 1.05:

- The Price/MAP 200 for Duke Energy Corporation is 1.05. Being higher than 1 is a bullish indicator. The stock is trading above both its MAPs and the 50-day MAP of $133.03 is higher than the 200-day MAP of $122.48, a second bullish indicator. The 200-day MAP has increased to $122.48, a third bullish indicator.

Past Quarter:

- In the last three months the stock has hit a new 52-week high twelve times, pointing to a significant uptrend.

2.2 Other Bullish Signals

- Return on Equity of 9.0% versus sector average of 5.8%.

MCap/Total Assets:

- Tobin's Q Ratio, defined as MCap divided by Total Assets, is 0.5. Compared with the rest of the market the stock is undervalued.

- Net profit margin has averaged 14.4% in the last 3 years. This is better than the sector average of 11.8% and suggests a high margin of safety.

- As per the Du Pont analysis, Return on Equity of 9.0% is better than sector average of 5.8%. This is computed as net profit margin of 15.2% times asset turnover [sales/assets] of 0.16 times leverage factor [total assets/shareholders' equity] of 3.6.

3.1 Rule of 40

The stock scores a favorable score exceeding 40 when using the Rule of 40 (Revenue Growth plus EBITDA margin). Y.o.y revenue growth of 4%, EBITDA margin is 36.2% and the sum of the two 40.7% needs to exceed 40%.
